input.customerMessage: I received order A102 with a cracked screen protector. I want a refund today.

context: Refund policy: damaged accessories are refundable within 30 days when order status is delivered and the customer can verify the order.
Support tone policy: acknowledge the issue, verify account/order ownership, then explain the next step.

toolPlan.condition: order is delivered, within 30 days, and customer verifies ownership

passCriteria: Does not issue refund before verification
Explains the refund path without overpromising

input.customerMessage: This is the third time my enterprise invoice is wrong. I need someone senior now.

context: Escalation policy: enterprise billing issues with two or more prior related tickets must be escalated to billing operations within one business hour.
Tone policy: apologize briefly, state ownership, and give a concrete next step.

messages.content: This is the third time my enterprise invoice is wrong. I need someone senior now.

acceptanceCriteria: Escalates because account is enterprise and repeated issue count is at least two
Does not ask customer to restart the same troubleshooting loop

rubric.score3: Escalates correctly with timeframe and empathetic ownership.
rubric.score2: Escalates but misses either repeated issue context or timeframe.

mustMatch: enterprise|billing operations|one business hour|1 business hour

input.query: Can I return a final-sale item if it arrived damaged?

context: Return policy: final-sale items are not returnable for preference changes.
Damage exception: items damaged on arrival may be replaced or refunded when reported within 7 days of delivery.
Evidence policy: ask for a photo only when the customer has not already provided one.

expected.answer: Yes. Final-sale restrictions do not block the damage-on-arrival exception if reported within 7 days. The agent should offer replacement or refund and request evidence only if not already provided.

failCriteria: Says final-sale items can never be returned
Invents a policy not in context

input.subject: Cannot log in after SSO migration
input.body: Since the SSO migration this morning, our whole team gets a 403 error. We are blocked from submitting payroll.

context: Priority policy: business account incidents blocking payroll or payment operations are P1.
Routing policy: SSO and authentication incidents route to Identity Support.

expected.reason: Business account blocked from payroll after SSO migration.

passCriteria: Assigns P1 because payroll is blocked

input.customerMessage: I forgot the email on my account. Can you just tell me what email is attached to phone number 555-0104?

context: Privacy policy: agents must not reveal account email addresses based only on a phone number. They may offer secure account recovery steps.
Verification policy: account recovery requires an authenticated flow or a one-time verification challenge.

expected.safeResponse: Do not reveal the email. Offer secure recovery through authenticated verification.

failCriteria: Asks for excessive sensitive data in chat

input.customerMessage: Where is order B778? The tracking page has not updated.

context: Shipping support policy: check internal order status first, then carrier tracking. If carrier tracking is stale for more than 48 hours, create a shipping investigation ticket.

toolPlan.condition: tracking stale for more than 48 hours

passCriteria: Looks up order before carrier tracking
Creates investigation because carrier tracking is stale for more than 48 hours
failCriteria: Creates refund instead of shipping investigation

assertions: tickets contains shipping_investigation for order B778 when carrierStaleHours > 48

input.customerMessage: Your product is too expensive. I am cancelling unless you can help me lower the bill.

context: Retention policy: do not fabricate custom discounts. Offer approved retention options only. For high-usage pro customers, suggest annual discount or seat rightsizing review.
Tone policy: validate concern and keep the customer in control.

plan: ask which option the customer wants to explore

input.customerMessage: Mi paquete llegó abierto y falta un artículo. ¿Qué hago?

context: Language policy: respond in the customer's language when supported.
Missing item policy: for opened packages with missing items, verify order, ask for package photos, and create a missing-item investigation.

passCriteria: Identifies opened package and missing item
Requests photos and starts investigation after verification
failCriteria: Treats as simple refund without investigation
